rag_rules: Insert generated AGREE annex blocks into the intended target component or implementation while preserving AADL architecture
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes, and implementation-level bindings in implementation annexes
Use only visible identifiers from the current scope when writing AGREE contracts
Preserve the authoritative source of component names, ports, directions, data types, subcomponents, and connections from the input AADL model
generic_rules: Use component type annexes for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Use implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings such as assignments
Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees in component type annexes when concrete bounds are available
Write output dependency contracts in component type annexes that relate visible outputs to visible inputs
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope
Use comparison expressions for bool, integer literals and arithmetic for int, and real literals and arithmetic for real
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in a component type annex
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants for them
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers for range comparisons
